rows.text:
Update on the tragic bus crash in Virginia: 

Five people are dead, including a 13-year-old girl and a 7-year-old boy, after the driver of a motorcoach slammed into stopped traffic on I-95. 

@FMCSA Administrator Derek Barrs and our investigators are on the ground at the crash site working with the @NTSB.  

Local police confirm the driver of this motorcoach — a man from China who became a U.S. citizen — doesn’t speak English. He received his commercial drivers license from New York State in 2024. 

Unacceptable. This is exactly why we are holding states’ accountable, enforcing the rules of the road, and cracking down on drivers who can’t speak English.

If you can’t be properly trained, read our road signs, or communicate with law enforcement, you have no business driving a bus. 

Our investigators are reviewing New York licensing records, training documentation, and the driver's history. Any company, trainer, or school that contributed to putting an unqualified driver on the road will face intense scrutiny.

We’ll share more updates soon. 

My prayers are with the loved ones of the innocent lives lost and those who were hurt in this horrific crime.

rows.text:
One of the problems we have in public policy is that wonks don’t give any consideration to how their ideas will collide with actual human behavior. Take our Trump Accounts. There are millions of parents who will in theory benefit enormously from this investment in their kids’ future, but the policy does no good if parents never find out how to sign up for it.

From day one of our administration we’ve tried to think outside the box on problems like this. That’s why Elon recruited the best and brightest engineers and designers to help us make government more efficient. Some of those same exceptional design and software talents, under @jgebbia’s leadership, have been working for months at National Design Studio on a different problem: making government programs like the Trump Accounts more user friendly.

With the Trump Accounts we’ve met people where they are. Instead of expecting them to navigate a ton of complicated forms on https://t.co/4MbCJUKklg, we’ve built out a simple, easy-to-use app to sign parents up. The app helps us market the program and sign up users, and deploying it this early does something else: it gives our engineers a pilot program to test out Treasury’s digital back-end systems and iron out kinks in advance of the delivery of the funds later this summer. So, parents, sign up now, and help us invest in your kids for tomorrow.

Because great policy only works when people can actually use it. That is the power of design. When government is easier to understand, easier to navigate, and easier to trust, people benefit. The American experience should be as great as the American promise.

rows.text:
🚨Mexican illegal alien charged with killing four Americans

Four young Americans – two who just graduated from high school – died May 22 after a head-on collision with a driver going the wrong direction on I-40 in Oklahoma that caused the car to catch fire.

The allegedly drunk suspect, Mexican illegal alien Michael Salomon Rosario-Cruz, faces charges including:

—4 counts of second-degree murder
—2 counts of DUI causing great bodily injury 
—Transporting an open container
—Possessing a firearm with an altered ID while committing a felony
—Carrying firearms while under the influence
—Wrong way on a one-way road

ICE filed an immigration detainer for Rosario-Cruz, who should never have been in the country to begin with, to ensure he’s never released to endanger others again.

rows.text:
🚨AMO Black Hawk disabling fire ends vessel pursuit🚨

On May 13, AMO aircrews detected a suspicious 25-foot Yola-type vessel northwest of Puerto Rico, carrying three individuals and visible packages. Our teams maintained continuous aerial surveillance, tracking the vessel through the night.

The next evening, the San Juan Marine Unit launched a coordinated interdiction operation. Two Coastal Interceptor Vessels and multiple aircraft moved in, guided by constant vectors from our aircrews.

At 9:51 p.m., the targeted vessel was intercepted north of San Juan. The suspects tried to evade capture and began dumping contraband into the water. In a decisive moment, the AMO Black Hawk crew deployed Air Disabling Fire, stopping the vessel cold.

Three Dominican Republic nationals were taken into custody, and five bales containing 391 lbs. of cocaine were seized and transferred to the Homeland Security Task Force. This operation highlights our relentless commitment to securing America’s borders and keeping dangerous drugs off our streets. 

Check out the full video here: https://t.co/F5r4WqBtKU 

#AMO #BorderSecurity #Teamwork

🇺🇸 The quarterly trade forecast confirms what we already knew: America’s farmers and ranchers are competing — and winning — again. 🚜

The agricultural trade deficit we inherited by the previous administration is down 42%! We remain steadfast and focused on getting back to a surplus.

And we’re just getting started. President Trump’s latest China agreement — which is not yet incorporated in the forecast — includes $30 BILLION of annual commitments to buy American #ag products (25MMT of soybeans + $17B of additional goods). 🌾🤝🇺🇸

At the same time, we’re diversifying and expanding markets worldwide and this year are forecasted to set RECORD EXPORTS to:
🇲🇽 Mexico
🇪🇺 EU
🇰🇷 South Korea
🇻🇳 Vietnam
🇨🇴 Colombia
🇹🇼 Taiwan
🇩🇴 Dominican Republic

rows.text:
Today is personal for me.
 
Before I ever had the privilege of leading this organization, I wore the green uniform and stood the line alongside the men and women of the U.S. Border Patrol. I know the sacrifices made behind the badge, the long nights, the missed moments with family, and the pride that comes with serving something greater than yourself.
 
For 102 years, Border Patrol agents have answered the call with courage, grit, and an unwavering commitment to protecting this nation. I could not be prouder to have been one of them.
 
Happy 102nd Birthday to the U.S. Border Patrol and to every agent, past and present, who continues to carry the mission forward. 

Honor First!
